mission.title: Install and Configure Valkey
mission.description: Valkey is a high-performance distributed key-value database optimized for caching and real-time workloads. It is suitable for applications requiring fast data access and supports a variety of data structures. Key trade-offs include the need for careful configuration and potential complexity in deployment.

steps.title: Add Valkey Helm Repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Valkey Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add valkey https://github.com/valkey-io/valkey/releases/download/v9.0.3

```

> This adds the repository for Valkey version 9.0.3.

steps.title: Install Valkey CRDs
steps.description:
This step installs the Custom Resource Definitions required for Valkey to function properly.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://github.com/valkey-io/valkey/releases/download/v9.0.3/valkey-crds.yaml

```

> This installs the necessary CRDs for Valkey.

steps.title: Install Valkey using Helm
steps.description:
This step installs Valkey into the specified namespace using Helm.

```bash

helm install valkey valkey/valkey --namespace valkey --version 9.0.3

```

> This installs Valkey in the 'valkey' namespace.

steps.title: Create Valkey Custom Resource
steps.description:
This step creates an instance of the Valkey custom resource to deploy the database.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: valkey.io/v1
kind: Valkey
metadata:
  name: my-valkey
spec:
  replicas: 3
EOF

```

> This creates a Valkey instance with 3 replicas.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: valkey.io/v1
kind: Valkey
metadata:
  name: my-valkey
spec:
  replicas: 3
EOF

steps.title: Verify Valkey Deployment
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the Valkey deployment to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n valkey

```

> This lists the pods in the Valkey namespace to verify they are running.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Valkey
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Valkey deployment and associated resources. Be aware that this will result in data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall valkey --namespace valkey

kubectl delete -f https://github.com/valkey-io/valkey/releases/download/v9.0.3/valkey-crds.yaml

```

> This removes the Valkey release.
> This deletes the CRDs associated with Valkey.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Valkey
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your data. This step will upgrade Valkey to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl cp my-valkey:/data /backup/my-valkey-backup

helm upgrade valkey valkey/valkey --namespace valkey --version 9.0.3

kubectl get pods -n valkey

```

> Backup your data from the Valkey pod.
> This upgrades Valkey to version 9.0.3.
> Verify that the upgraded pods are running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to Valkey server'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Valkey server may not be running or is misconfigured.

**Fix:**
Check the pod status with 'kubectl get pods -n valkey' and ensure the service is correctly configured.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient resources to schedule pod'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster may not have enough resources available.

**Fix:**
Scale up your cluster or reduce the resource requests in your Valkey deployment.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Valkey CRD not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Custom Resource Definition for Valkey may not have been installed.

**Fix:**
Reapply the CRDs using 'kubectl apply -f https://github.com/valkey-io/valkey/releases/download/v9.0.3/valkey-crds.yaml'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Pod is CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Valkey pod may be crashing due to misconfiguration or resource limits.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n valkey' to diagnose the issue.

versionNotes.changes: Introduced enhanced performance optimizations for key-value operations and improved plugin support.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated support for older TLS versions.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from version 8.x, ensure to review the new configuration options for plugins.

resolution.summary: You should see the Valkey pods running with a status of 'Running' when executing 'kubectl get pods -n valkey'.
codeSnippets:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: valkey.io/v1
kind: Valkey
metadata:
  name: my-valkey
spec:
  replicas: 3
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster set up and accessible.
